metadata.assumptions: ~50 men per ship; scholarly convention. Homer does not give the exact number.
metadata.primary_source: Homer. The Odyssey. Translated by Emily Wilson. Norton, 2017.
metadata.x_axis: episode_order (1-9, narrative sequence, not chronological time)
metadata.study_period_note: The study period ends when the last sailor dies (E009). The ~7 years with Calypso are excluded: no crew at risk.

events.source_note: 6 men per ship × 12 ships; the 72 casualties follow disobeying Odysseus's order to retreat (Wilson Od.9.43-61); see SOURCES.md §E001
0 deaths; included as a risk episode; Wilson Od.9.84-97

events.location: Polyphemus's cave, island of the Cyclopes
events.source_note: 2 men devoured each night × 3 nights = 6; only Odysseus's ship enters; Wilson Od.9.289-344
0 direct deaths; crew opens the bag of winds despite an explicit prohibition (disobedience); Wilson Od.10.27-55
11 of 12 ships destroyed with their entire crews; 11 × 44 men/ship (estimate; see SOURCES.md §E005); Wilson Od.10.80-132
Men turned into pigs but restored; 0 permanent deaths; Wilson Od.10.210-243
Elpenor falls from the roof drunk before departure; Wilson Od.10.552-560
Scylla takes exactly 6 men, one per head; number confirmed in the text; Wilson Od.12.245-259
Crew slaughters the cattle despite an explicit prohibition (disobedience); Zeus destroys the ship; 31 aboard, 30 die, Odysseus censored; Wilson Od.12.340-419

humanos_hostiles.note: raw count, without adjustment for differential exposure time between categories

monstruo.note: raw count, without adjustment for differential exposure time between categories

dios.note: raw count, without adjustment for differential exposure time between categories

accidente.note: raw count, without adjustment for differential exposure time between categories

disobedience_comparison.methodological_note: Descriptive comparison only. No log-rank test is applied: failure times within each mass-casualty episode are not independent (Laestrygonians E005: 484 simultaneous deaths; Helios E009: 30 simultaneous). A confidence interval on this data would be decorative rigor. See README §Limitations.
